skill.name: firecrawl-build-search
skill.description: Integrate Firecrawl `/search` into product code and agent workflows. Use when an app needs discovery before extraction, when the feature starts with a query instead of a URL, or when the system should search the web and optionally hydrate result content.
skill.summary: Web search API integration for product discovery workflows
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: All 25 static findings are false positives. The file is a markdown documentation file that describes Firecrawl API integration. Static scanner incorrectly flagged markdown backtick syntax as Ruby code, hardcoded URLs as suspicious network calls, relative markdown paths as path traversal, and YAML metadata as code patterns. No malicious patterns confirmed after semantic evaluation.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged markdown backtick syntax as Ruby code execution. Backticks are used for inline code formatting (e.g., `/search`). No Ruby or shell commands present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick syntax in markdown is standard code formatting. Lines contain documentation text referencing API endpoints, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs - False Positive
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged documentation URLs as suspicious. These are legitimate links to official Firecrawl documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to firecrawl.dev and github.com official domains. Standard documentation references.

low_findings.title: Python HTTP Libraries - False Positive
low_findings.description: Scanner misidentified YAML metadata as Python HTTP library usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line contains YAML description field for API key input, not Python code.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Reference - False Positive
low_findings.description: FIRECRAWL_API_KEY is a legitimate environment variable name in YAML frontmatter.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard YAML input definition for API key configuration. No secret value exposed.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequences - False Positive
low_findings.description: Scanner misidentified markdown relative paths as filesystem path traversal attacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines contain markdown links to related skill documentation files using standard relative paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
low_findings.description: Scanner incorrectly identified text patterns as cryptographic algorithm references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic code exists in this documentation file. Scanner incorrectly analyzed text content as code.

content.user_title: Add Firecrawl search to your product
content.value_statement: Applications that start with a user query need web discovery before extraction. This skill provides the integration pattern for using Firecrawl `/search` to turn queries into ranked results. It reduces development time and ensures proper API usage for product teams building search-powered features.
actual_capabilities: Integrate Firecrawl `/search` endpoint into application code
Transform user queries into ranked web results
Handle search result metadata and URL extraction
Configure API credentials for Firecrawl integration
Follow Firecrawl documentation patterns for multiple languages
limitations: Does not perform actual web scraping (use firecrawl-build-scrape for that)
Search quality depends on Firecrawl service availability
No built-in caching or rate limiting for production use

use_cases.title: Research and discovery tools
use_cases.description: Build applications that answer user questions by first discovering relevant sources, then extracting content from top results.
use_cases.target_user: Product teams building research assistants or knowledge tools

use_cases.title: Competitive analysis dashboards
use_cases.description: Create pipelines that search for company or topic information and surface ranked results for further analysis.
use_cases.target_user: Business intelligence and market research teams

use_cases.title: Content aggregation workflows
use_cases.description: Use search as the first step in pipelines that collect content from multiple sources for aggregation or summarization.

prompt_templates.title: Basic search integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add web search to my product. The user enters a query and I want to show them ranked results. How do I integrate Firecrawl search?
prompt_templates.scenario: When starting a new search feature in an application

prompt_templates.title: Search with result extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to use Firecrawl search to find sources, then extract key content from the top results.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-language support
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to use Firecrawl search in a Python/TypeScript project. Show me the recommended integration pattern for my language.
prompt_templates.scenario: When integrating with a specific programming language

prompt_templates.title: Production deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the best practices for deploying Firecrawl search in production? I need error handling and API key management.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing a search feature for production release

output_examples.input: User asks: 'What are the latest developments in AI safety?'
output_examples.output: Your product returns a ranked list of relevant web sources with titles, snippets, and URLs that the user can click to explore further.

output_examples.input: User searches for competitor information on a topic
output_examples.output: The application surfaces top results from multiple sources, enabling the user to compare perspectives before deeper research.

output_examples.input: Research query that requires up-to-date information
output_examples.output: The search endpoint returns current web results with metadata, which your application can display while reserving extraction for selected results.

best_practices: Use search for discovery before scraping. Keep these operations conceptually separate unless you need to scrape search results.
Treat search as ranking and source selection. Be explicit about whether you need snippets, URLs, or full result content.
Store your FIRECRAWL_API_KEY securely in environment variables and never commit it to version control.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys directly in source code. Always use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Avoid scraping all search results by default. Be selective to manage cost and latency.
Do not skip the documentation phase. Follow the language-specific patterns in Firecrawl docs for reliable integration.

faq.question: What is the difference between Firecrawl search and scrape?
faq.answer: Search discovers and ranks URLs based on a query. Scrape extracts content from specific URLs. Use search first when you do not have a specific URL.

faq.question: Do I need a Firecrawl API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Set the FIRECRAWL_API_KEY environment variable with your Firecrawl API key before using the search endpoint.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is designed for integration into any AI agent workflow, including Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What happens if the search returns no results?
faq.answer: Handle empty results gracefully in your application. Display a user-friendly message and consider alternative queries or fallback options.

faq.question: How do I combine search with scraping?
faq.answer: Use search first to get ranked URLs, then pass the URLs to the firecrawl-build-scrape skill for content extraction.

faq.question: Is there a rate limit for Firecrawl search?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your Firecrawl plan. Check the Firecrawl documentation for your specific rate limit tier.
